screens.title: Time to look at your neighbourhood again
description: Ten years have passed since you moved to this neighbourhood. The road in front of the bus stop where you take your daily bus to work has been in poor condition. It was dug up last month for laying pipes. It is still the same. You see buses tilting and two-wheelers almost toppling on the broken road.
Residents from your neighbourhood decide to get it fixed.
What will you do?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Time to look at your neighbourhood again

actions.label: Encourage the residents to go ahead
Tell them there is no point

screens.title: A Broken Road, A Broken Leg
description: You continue using the broken road. One day, you trip on a stone and break your leg. You miss work for many weeks and spend ₹10,000 on medical care.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for A Broken Road, A Broken Leg

actions.label: Complain to the residents petitioning for a better road
You don't have time to engage

screens.title: You Move On
description: Your leg heals. You’re more careful now. You know which patches to avoid. The others are still at it. You wish them luck. Maybe it’ll work out, maybe it won’t.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for You Move On

screens.title: About It
description: This journey has been developed to showcase systemic challenges citizens face to fix a broken road. Janaagraha has been advocating for many reforms to solve these challenges.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for About It

screens.title: Invited to the group
description: You reach out to the residents' group. They invite you to join them on this journey!
What would you do?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Invited to the group

actions.label: Just say that you have faced the issue and leave for work

screens.title: The Residents Fill You In
description: The residents are thinking of approaching the Councillor.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Residents Fill You In

screens.title: Two Weeks Have Passed
description: Two weeks in, and nothing has changed.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Two Weeks Have Passed

actions.label: Check in with the residents' group

screens.title: You Cheered Them On
description: You cheered the group on and hoped they would succeed. Two weeks later, the road is still broken.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for You Cheered Them On

actions.label: Check in with the residents' group

screens.title: Still Waiting on the MLA
description: The group approached the MLA but hasn't heard back.
How can you help them?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Still Waiting on the MLA

actions.label: Suggest they approach the Councillor instead

screens.title: Word on the Street
description: Your neighbour says the MLA will fix the road but he's not part of the group, so you can't be sure.
Talk to someone from the group?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Word on the Street

actions.label: No, carry on with the broken road

screens.title: The Group Comes Together
description: Ten residents have come together. You meet to decide on a plan of action.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Group Comes Together

revealBlocks.text: What plan of action will you recommend?

screens.title: Out of the MLA's Hands
description: The MLA hears you out but says road repairs fall under the Municipal Corporation. He's not the right person for this.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Out of the MLA's Hands

actions.label: Seek help from the MLA to reach out to the Councillor

screens.title: Where Is the Councillor?
description: The group sets out to meet the Councillor, but nobody knows the exact location of their office.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Where Is the Councillor?

actions.label: You search for the office address online
Visit the Municipal office for help

screens.title: Complaint Registered, No Follow-Up
description: Your complaint is registered online. But there's no acknowledgement or follow-up.
What would you do next?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Complaint Registered, No Follow-Up

screens.title: Connected at Last
description: The MLA puts you in touch with the Councillor directly.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Connected at Last

screens.title: Wrong Address
description: You find an address online, but the Councillor doesn't sit there. Someone else may know more.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Wrong Address

actions.label: Meet the residents' group for more information

screens.title: The Group Has a Suggestion
description: The group doesn't know either, but someone suggests trying the municipal office for the address.
It's a holiday today. The group decides to visit the office tomorrow. Will you join them?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Group Has a Suggestion

actions.label: Visit the municipal office with others

screens.title: The Municipal Office to the rescue
description: Unlike the internet, the municipal office staff have the right details. You now know how to reach the Councillor.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Municipal Office to the rescue

screens.title: You Finally Reach the Councillor
description: You arrive at the Councillor's office, but they're unavailable today. They promise to meet with you on Sunday.
What will you do?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for You Finally Reach the Councillor

screens.title: The Councillor Hears You Out
description: You return on Sunday. The Councillor listens and says she'll inform the Assistant Engineer (AE).
Will you wait for the AE to visit your neighbourhood?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Councillor Hears You Out

actions.label: Wait a few days for the AE's visit

screens.title: Silence after AE visit
description: The AE visits, assesses the damage, takes notes, and leaves. Days pass. Nothing happens.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Silence after AE visit

actions.label: Go back to the Councillor's office

screens.title: Try the Ward Office
description: The Councillor asks you to follow up at the Ward Office.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Try the Ward Office

screens.title: The Group Loses Hope
description: The AE never comes back after his visit. No one knows what happened. The group has quietly disbanded.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Group Loses Hope

screens.title: Progress at the Ward Office
description: At the Ward Office, the AE says workers are unavailable but promises the job will be done within a week. A few days later, a team from the Municipality arrives and patches sections of the road.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Progress at the Ward Office

actions.label: Express concern about the quality of patchwork
Rejoice — At least the road is half fixed!

revealBlocks.text: You can see parts of the road are still broken. What will you do now?

screens.title: Nature’s verdict on the patchwork
description: Your concern was well-founded. A few days later, the patchwork washes away in the rain. You return to the Councillor, who summons the AE.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Nature’s verdict on the patchwork

screens.title: Nature’s verdict on the patchwork
description: It seemed like progress. Then the rain comes. A few days later, the patchwork washes away. You return to the Councillor, who summons the AE.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Nature’s verdict on the patchwork

screens.title: The Problem Is Bigger
description: The AE says a proper fix needs far more funding than is available; the matter is beyond his authority. He asks you to take it up with the Councillor.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Problem Is Bigger

actions.label: Demand that the Councillor secure the necessary funds

screens.title: How to get the funds
description: The Councillor says the decision isn't hers to make. It must go before the Standing Committee — and that will take time.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for How to get the funds

actions.label: Demand that the matter be sent to the Standing Committee
Do nothing — everyone drops the matter

screens.title: Standing Committee
description: The Councillor agrees to forward your request to the Standing Committee.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Standing Committee

actions.label: Do nothing — everyone drops the matter

revealBlocks.text: Many days pass. You have no idea if there has been any progress.

screens.title: Rejection
description: The Councillor admits she hasn't had a chance to raise it yet — but promises to do so today. After several follow-ups, you learn the Standing Committee has rejected the request, citing a shortage of funds. The proposal is deferred indefinitely.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Rejection

actions.label: Approach the Mayor or Municipal Commissioner
Ask the Councillor to push harder
Do nothing — everyone drops the matter

screens.title: The Mayor Steps in
description: The Mayor hears you out and agrees to speak to the Standing Committee on your behalf. Your group sees a ray of hope. It has been a few days since the Mayor agreed. Wait a little longer?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Mayor Steps in

screens.title: Still Waiting
description: The Councillor says she'll try to raise the matter again when the time is right. Will you agree?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Still Waiting

screens.title: No Answers
description: You approach a Standing Committee member but get no satisfactory response. You've spent many days following up. What else can you do?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for No Answers

actions.label: Protest in front of the Municipal Corporation office

screens.title: All The Same
description: The other members are no different. You've hit a dead end. You begin to wonder - is it even possible to fix this road?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for All The Same

screens.title: Funds approved
description: The Standing Committee approves the fund and agrees to treat this as priority.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Funds approved

revealBlocks.text: REJOICE! You made it happen. You head home hopeful that your road will finally be fixed — and that you will soon be able to walk it without fear.

screens.title: Who will do it
description: Weeks pass and nothing moves. The Councillor and AE tell you a contractor is needed — but no one has responded to the tender. The funds aren't enough to attract bids.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Who will do it

actions.label: Offer to organize the work yourself
Approach the MLA for additional support
Approach the Mayor or Municipal Commissioner
Approach the Standing Committee members again

screens.title: More Funds
description: The Corporation releases additional funds. The tender attracts a contractor, and the road is fixed within a couple of weeks. Life on the road is safe again!
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for More Funds

screens.title: You Can't Do It Yourself
description: You give it your best effort, but you don’t have time to deal with the complexity – you have other things which need your attention.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for You Can't Do It Yourself

actions.label: Meet the Commissioner or the Standing Committee

screens.title: The MLA Comes Through
description: This time, the MLA listens. He arranges for the necessary funds to be sanctioned.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The MLA Comes Through

actions.label: Use the MLA funds to fix the road

screens.title: Test of time
description: You did it. Your persistence paid off, and the road is finally fixed.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Test of time

actions.label: Start again and try a different path

revealBlocks.text: But a few months later, the sewage department digs up the road to lay pipes for an upcoming real-estate project. Nobody tells you whether it will be restored. What will you do now?

screens.title: What Would You Choose?
description: Thank you for stepping up. Your perseverance made the road safer for all. You don't want to go through this again and you don't want other citizens to face it either. What would you do to solve the challenges you faced?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for What Would You Choose?

actions.label: Plan better for roads and utilities to avoid repeated digging
Hire more skilled personnel in the ULB
Give the Municipal Corporation more funds for maintenance
Give the Councillors more power and accountability
Better coordination between the ULB, sewage department, and other agencies

screens.title: Reform: Better Infrastructure Planning
description: The city adopts road designs that accommodate utility work without repeated digging. Once built to this standard, disruption would be far less frequent. However, this needs significantly more funding and budget approvals.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Reform: Better Infrastructure Planning

screens.title: Reform: Skilled Staff in the ULB
description: With skilled staff, the AE can tell you upfront that patchwork won't last and the ULB can do proper repairs without always relying on contractors. But the sewage department still digs up the road, pointing to a coordination problem this reform alone can't fix.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Reform: Skilled Staff in the ULB

screens.title: Reform: More Funds for the Municipal Corporation
description: With more funds, the Corporation sanctions your repair much sooner. But the sewage department still digs up the road — funding alone can't bridge every gap.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Reform: More Funds for the Municipal Corporation

screens.title: Reform: Empowering Councillors
description: With stronger roles in local decision-making, elected representatives could move everyday issues like road repairs forward with fewer procedural hurdles.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Reform: Empowering Councillors

screens.title: Reform: Better Inter-Departmental Coordination
description: You still go through the full Standing Committee process, but this time, the road is built only after the sewage pipes are already in place. You wait longer, but the road is less likely to be dug up again soon.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Reform: Better Inter-Departmental Coordination

screens.title: Thank You for Playing
description: You shuttled between offices, chased funds, and fought bureaucracy only for the road to be dug up again. No single reform fixes this. Only when they work together can our cities truly improve. That's the City Systems approach.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for Thank You for Playing

screens.title: And It Happens Again
description: Days later, a motorcycle hits a pothole. Two people are badly injured. It makes the news briefly, then fades. The road stays broken.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for And It Happens Again

actions.label: Get back in and fight the good fight

screens.title: The Matter Is Dropped
description: Without follow-up, the issue fades. Residents move on. The road stays broken.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for The Matter Is Dropped

actions.label: There has to be a better way - explore reforms

screens.title: When We Walk Away
description: Civic problems take time. But ignored roads stay dangerous for everyone who uses them. Staying engaged keeps the pressure on.
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for When We Walk Away

screens.title: What Could Be Done Differently?
description: You stepped away and that’s okay. These problems can be difficult to navigate. But lasting change usually needs systemic changes. What would you focus on?
screens.imageAlt: Story illustration for What Could Be Done Differently?

actions.label: Plan better for roads and utilities to avoid repeated digging
Hire more skilled personnel in the ULB
Give the Municipal Corporation more funds for maintenance
Give the Councillors more power and accountability
Better coordination between the ULB, sewage department, and other agencies
